title: SACRAMENTO, CA—
prompt_preview:

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California needs a comprehensive strategy to address the fentanyl crisis.",
    "The Assembly has passed more fentanyl legislation than any time in its history.",
    "The Select Committee on Fentanyl is exploring root causes and developing policies to combat the fentanyl crisis.",
    "There are other illicit drugs on the horizon that will continue to devastate California.",
    "The fentanyl crisis will continue to grow if governments do not work together to stop it.",
    "The Select Committee on Fentanyl started the work of bringing together law enforcement, public health experts, and families affected by the crisis.",
    "Legislation has been passed that increases punishment for high-level fentanyl dealers.",
    "Legislation has been passed that invests in fentanyl and opioid education for parents and teachers.",
    "Legislation has expanded access to treatment that rapidly reverses overdoses or detects fentanyl.",
    "Community colleges and CSU are required to stock and distribute fentanyl test strips.",
    "The State Threat Assessment Center and the Office of Emergency Services are required to prioritize cooperation to dismantle criminal networks trafficking opioid drugs.",
    "Weight enhancements that increase penalties for trafficking certain substances now apply to fentanyl.",
    "Social media platforms are required to disclose their policies regarding communications between users, aiding law enforcement in investigating online illicit drug transactions.",
    "Health plans and Medi-Cal are required to cover prescription and over-the-counter Narcan.",
    "Good samaritans administering opioid antagonists are not typically liable for civil damages.",
    "Schools must include a protocol for responding to a student's opioid overdose in their safety plans.",
    "A court order can be sought to require a social media platform to remove content that offers to sell controlled substances.",
    "Public places must maintain unexpired doses of opioid antagonists and ensure that staff are aware of their locations.",
    "The state is required to make all FDA-approved formulations of naloxone available to certain applicants."
  ]
}
```
